Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple A basic cyber attack targeting transportation systems involves initial access through phishing, followed by execution via user interaction, establishing persistence by running a malicious file, and finally causing impact by destroying data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Employee Endpoints, Network Infrastructure

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers send phishing emails to employees, tricking them into clicking on a link leading to malicious content.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-663: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: The victim clicks on the link, downloading a malicious ZIP archive which they then execute, unknowingly triggering the payload.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: After gaining access, Pikabot is used to delete critical files or services, disrupting the transportation system operations.

Scenario.summary: This simplistic cyber attack leverages phishing to gain entry into the targeted transportation systems. Once executed, the malware persists and ultimately causes significant impact by destructively altering critical system files.
